---
title: Getting Started
---

## Installation

### Using pip

```bash
pip install pycmdcheck
```

### Using uv

```bash
uv add pycmdcheck
```

### Optional Dependencies

pycmdcheck can use external tools for linting and type checking. Install the optional dependencies based on your needs:

```bash
# For mypy type checking
pip install pycmdcheck[typing-mypy]

# For pyright type checking
pip install pycmdcheck[typing-pyright]

# For ruff linting
pip install pycmdcheck[linting]

# All optional dependencies
pip install pycmdcheck[all]
```

## Your First Check

### Using the CLI

Navigate to your Python package directory and run:

```bash
pycmdcheck
```

You'll see output like (abbreviated — a default run executes all 20 checks):

```
Checking package: /path/to/your/package

┏━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
┃ St… ┃ Check           ┃ Message                     ┃
┡━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┩
│  ✓  │ metadata        │ Package metadata is valid   │
│  ✓  │ structure       │ Valid src layout structure  │
│  ✓  │ license         │ License file present        │
│  ✓  │ docs            │ Documentation present       │
│  ✓  │ imports         │ All imports are valid       │
│  ✓  │ tests           │ All tests passed            │
│  ✓  │ linting         │ No linting issues found     │
│  ✓  │ typing          │ No type errors found        │
│  ✓  │ version         │ Version 0.1.0 is consistent │
│  ℹ  │ ci              │ No CI configuration found   │
│  …  │ …               │ … (all 20 checks run)       │
└─────┴─────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┘

18 ok | 2 note

All checks passed!
```

### Using Python

```python
from pycmdcheck import check

# Check current directory
report = check(".")

# Print summary
print(f"Passed: {report.passed}")
print(f"Results: {len(report.results)} checks run")

# Iterate over results
for result in report.results:
    print(f"{result.status.symbol} {result.name}: {result.message}")
```

## Understanding Check Results

Each check returns one of five statuses:

| Status | Symbol | Description |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| OK | ✓ | Check passed successfully |
| NOTE | ℹ | Informational message, not a problem |
| WARNING | ⚠ | Potential issue, should be reviewed |
| ERROR | ✗ | Check failed |
| SKIPPED | ○ | Check was skipped (e.g., tool not installed) |

## Built-in Checks

pycmdcheck includes 20 built-in checks:

| Check | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `metadata` | Validate package metadata (pyproject.toml) |
| `structure` | Validate package directory structure |
| `tests` | Run package tests (pytest/unittest) |
| `linting` | Run code linting (ruff/flake8/pylint) |
| `typing` | Run type checking (mypy/pyright) |
| `imports` | Validate package imports |
| `license` | Check for license file |
| `docs` | Check documentation (README, docstrings) |
| `dependencies` | Audit declared vs. actual dependencies |
| `build` | Verify package builds successfully |
| `formatting` | Check code formatting (ruff format/black) |
| `version` | Verify version consistency |
| `py_typed` | Check for PEP 561 py.typed marker |
| `community` | Check for community health files |
| `ci` | Check for CI configuration |
| `changelog` | Check for changelog or news file |
| `citation` | Check for citation file |
| `python_versions` | Check Python version support (EOL versions) |
| `urls` | Validate project URLs are reachable |
| `doctests` | Run doctests in package modules |

See [Configuration](configuration.qmd) for per-check options and
[CLI Reference](cli.qmd) for profiles that select subsets of these.

## Next Steps

- [CLI Reference](cli.qmd) - Learn all CLI options
- [Configuration](configuration.qmd) - Configure checks in pyproject.toml
- [Custom Checks](custom-checks.qmd) - Create your own checks
